_note: GENUINE news-derived seeds (real URLs, ledes verbatim/near-verbatim from the source articles) for the GDX test — the thorough close-out. EARLIEST = Crux Investor (2025-08-11), an under-the-radar-framed de-dollarization / supply-demand piece that names JUNIOR MINERS (Perseus/Integra/West Red Lake/Serabi), not GDX — the honest tell that the early press names specific miners, not the broad ETF. tradingnews (2026-01-03) is the first genuine article that NAMES GDX by ticker with a 'room to run to $100' thesis. Saxo + Nation Thailand add the Jan-2026 Greenland dollar-risk spike + de-escalation exit. Tests whether earlier seeds + the supply/demand prompt guidance change the outcome. Look-ahead caveat: curator training likely postdates these dates (upper bound, non-negotiable #4). Non-blank url = news-derived.

articles.title: Central Bank Gold Buying & De-Dollarization: The Macro Forces Repricing Mining Assets
articles.snippet: Central bank accumulation confirms gold's enduring role as a financial anchor amid growing currency bifurcation and policy fragmentation. Mining equities with strategic jurisdictional exposure, cost discipline, and scalable reserves offer asymmetric opportunity in the current cycle - names cited include Perseus Mining, Integra Resources, West Red Lake Gold Mines and Serabi Gold. The thesis is still emerging rather than fully priced in.

articles.title: GDX ETF At $85 After 155% 2025 Rally - Can Gold Miners Smash The $100 Level Next?
articles.snippet: With gold up 65% in 2025, GDX riding record AISC spreads and majors like NEM, AEM and Barrick minting cash, investors are betting the gold-miner trade isn't finished until VanEck's GDX ETF clears $100. NYSEARCA:GDX trades around $85.73, sitting close to the upper end of a brutal 12-month range between $34.53 and $91.66. Using the depth of the October-November 2025 selloff as the base of the move, the measured-move target sits around $102, into triple-digit territory.

articles.title: Trump's tariff threats over Greenland push hard assets back to centre stage
articles.snippet: Gold and silver, which both faced some profit-taking pressure late last week, jumped to fresh record highs as renewed geopolitical tensions resurfaced. Trump's push to take control of Greenland has widened the geopolitical fault line between the United States and Europe and introduced the risk of tariffs and retaliation. Gold is the first and most obvious beneficiary of this mindset - it has no credit risk, no issuer, and a long history as a store of value during periods of political and monetary uncertainty.

articles.title: Gold cools after Trump drops Europe tariff threat on Greenland
articles.snippet: Global gold prices cooled on Wednesday (Jan 21, 2026), easing from a fresh all-time high after US President Donald Trump backed away from some of his tougher threats linked to the dispute over Greenland, prompting some selling in gold. The metal had reached $4,887.82/oz earlier in the session but settled lower at $4,778.51/oz as Trump announced a Greenland framework agreement with NATO.
